response.justification: Strong individual-contributor background with 13+ years of polyglot software engineering, testing advocacy, workshops, and some tooling work (CI/CD setup, test frameworks like Trial and Fluent-Asserts). However, the resume shows no formal people-management experience, no explicit team-leadership title, and no mobile-platform or build-system/developer-productivity org leadership at scale. The role requires 2+ years people management and 3+ years technical leadership in developer tools at an organizational level, which is not clearly demonstrated. Worth a screening call to probe leadership scope, but a direct fit for a Level II Engineering Manager role is uncertain.
strengths: Deep, broad engineering experience (web, mobile, embedded, backend) with testing and TDD focus
Demonstrated developer-tooling contributions: test runner (Trial), assertion library, CI/CD setup, MCP/agent tooling
Track record of teaching, workshops, and knowledge sharing — useful for team development
concerns: No explicit people-management role or headcount/team-lead title shown on resume
Limited evidence of mobile-platform ecosystem ownership or large-scale build-system work
No clear experience leading organizational change or owning a mid-term roadmap at team-scope
